master_row.title: Toroidal Boundary Condition: ∫∫_T^2 K DA
master_row.canonical_statement: This configuration forces satisfaction of the Gauss-Bonnet theorem. For a torus, the integrated curvature must satisfy: \begin{equation} \int\int_{\mathbb{T}^2} K\,dA = 2\pi\chi(\mathbb{T}^2) = 0 \end{equation}
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Toroidal Boundary Condition’.
master_row.dictionary: This configuration forces satisfaction of the Gauss-Bonnet theorem.
master_row.encyclopedia: Toroidal Boundary Condition: ∫∫_T^2 K DA is an integral expression, written ∫∫_T^2 K dA = 2πχ(T^2) = 0. It is obtained by integrating over a domain, accumulating contributions across the region of interest. It is built from π, χ, defining ∫∫_T^2 K dA. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formal_status.note: Proof package captures the theorem row, metadata, source truth, TeX source, and current semantic translation status.
